name: TREC Community Investors
description_short: TREC Community Investors is a Dallas-based nonprofit loan fund providing flexible real estate capital and technical assistance to nonprofits and for-profit developers in underserved neighborhoods.
description_long:
TREC Community Investors is a nonprofit organization headquartered in Dallas that functions as a community development loan fund rather than a traditional bank. Founded to address the gap in financing for real estate projects in disinvested neighborhoods, TREC works exclusively in Dallas and Collin counties with a mission to revitalize communities through equitable development and access to capital.

The organization offers three primary loan products: predevelopment loans (up to $750,000) covering early-stage costs like appraisals, architectural and engineering fees, and environmental assessments; acquisition loans for purchasing and securing land for future development; and construction loans financing the building or renovation phase. TREC also provides grants to qualifying nonprofits, commercial real estate consulting services delivered pro bono by volunteer professionals, and educational programs including Real Estate Bootcamp and Real Estate 101 for Nonprofits.

TREC distinguishes itself by explicitly accepting projects that conventional lenders deem too risky due to lack of comparable projects or insufficient collateral from borrowers. As a loan fund rather than a bank, TREC can extend financing to developers aligned with community development goals and demonstrating minimal repayment risk. The organization pairs capital access with technical expertise, reflecting its commitment to sustainable community transformation rather than extractive lending.

Prospective borrowers should understand that TREC serves a specific geographic footprint (Dallas and Collin counties) and prioritizes nonprofit and for-profit developers working in underserved areas. While the organization addresses genuine financing gaps, loan decisions depend on project alignment with TREC's community development mission and borrower creditworthiness. Interested applicants should contact Loan Underwriting Manager Clinton Hill directly with project details.
pros: Flexible loan products (predevelopment, acquisition, construction) specifically designed for high-risk real estate projects conventional lenders reject
Up to $750,000 in capital available per loan with opportunity to exceed through partnerships, reducing need for multiple funding sources
Market-rate interest with only 1% origination fee, making financing more affordable than predatory alternatives
Integrated technical assistance: pro bono commercial real estate consulting from accomplished volunteer professionals included with loans
Educational programming (Real Estate Bootcamp, Real Estate 101 for Nonprofits) helps borrowers build capacity, not just access capital
Explicit commitment to underserved neighborhoods and equity-focused development distinguishes from profit-only lenders
Grants available to nonprofits participating in community transformation, supplementing loan funding
cons: Geographic limitation: only serves Dallas and Collin counties, excluding borrowers in other Texas regions
Nonprofit structure means limited funding availability and potentially longer approval timelines compared to commercial banks
Requires alignment with TREC's community development mission; standard commercial projects may not qualify
Limited public information about specific loan terms, rates, approval criteria, and timeline—applicants must contact directly
Predevelopment loans capped at $750,000 maximum, inadequate for large-scale projects despite partnership potential
best_for: Nonprofit organizations seeking capital for community-focused real estate projects in Dallas/Collin County underserved areas
For-profit developers committed to equitable community development in neighborhoods conventional lenders avoid
Real estate projects considered 'risky' by traditional banks due to limited comparables or borrower collateral constraints
Borrowers valuing technical expertise and community partnership alongside financing
diagnosis: TREC Community Investors is best for nonprofit organizations and socially-motivated for-profit developers seeking real estate financing in Dallas/Collin County underserved neighborhoods where conventional lenders decline. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation and mission-alignment requirements—this is a community development fund, not a general commercial lender, so standard business real estate projects in affluent areas will not qualify.
services: Predevelopment loans covering appraisals, architectural/engineering fees, environmental assessments, legal, title/zoning, feasibility studies
Acquisition loans for purchasing and securing land for future development
Construction loans for building and renovation project financing
Grant funding for nonprofits participating in community transformation initiatives
Commercial Real Estate Consulting Services delivered pro bono by volunteer professionals
Real Estate 101 for Nonprofits educational program
Technical assistance paired with loan financing
Dallas Catalyst Project initiatives (Mill City, Forest District)
Dallas Community Land Trust program participation
Dallas Collaborative for Equitable Development partnership
